title: FAQ
description: Short answers to common you-agent-factory questions that point at the owning canonical docs for install, run, configuration, MCP, and dynamic workflows.
openingSummary: FAQ is the you-agent-factory short-answer surface for common product questions. Each reply stays brief and sends you to the canonical doc that owns the full contract.

whatItCovers.title: What It Covers
whatItCovers.body: FAQ is the you-agent-factory short-answer surface for common product and how-to questions about install, first run, configuration, MCP, and dynamic workflows. Use it when you need a scannable question, a short reply, and a link into the owning canonical doc rather than a second copy of that doc.

keyConcepts.title: Key Concepts
keyConcepts.body: An FAQ entry pairs a common question with a short reply and a pointer to the canonical documentation page that owns the deeper contract. Answers stay link-forward: read the short guidance first, then open what-is, getting started, install, CLI, configuration, MCP, dynamic workflows, or Troubleshooting when you need the full reference. This surface does not replace those pages.

howToUse.title: How To Use
howToUse.body: Scan for the question that matches what you need, read the short reply, then follow the link into the owning canonical doc when you need commands, flags, or deeper setup. Use this page for common-question lookup. Open the linked what-is, getting-started, install, CLI, configuration, MCP, dynamic-workflows, or Troubleshooting pages when you need the full contract those surfaces own.

limitsAndAssumptions.title: Limits And Assumptions
limitsAndAssumptions.body: This page is a web FAQ for common you-agent-factory questions. It is not the install command matrix, not the getting-started quickstart, not a full CLI flag dump, not the configuration or global-configuration-factories deep dive, not MCP host setup, not dynamic-workflow authoring, and not a sync of packaged you docs. Sibling links aid discovery only; they are not required to understand the short replies here.
